The genus was named after the German horticulturist and botanist Johann Christoph Buxbaum's teacher, Johann Baptist Zaluziansky von Zaluzien. This genus consists of only one species, Zaluzianskia dasyphylla. Zaluzianskia dasyphylla is a shrubby perennial herb with yellow flowers.

intro: Zaluzianskia (Zaluzianskia dombeyana), also known as the Dombeya or the Rose Mallow, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to Madagascar, characterized by its showy flowers and edible fruits. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, which can aid in treating various ailments, including fever, diarrhea, and respiratory issues.
